criteria.evidence: `python tools/wordfreq.py tools/tests/sample.txt 5` -> 5 lines: 'the 6 / fox 5 / dog 4 / bird 3 / cat 3', exit=0. Independent recount of sample.txt gives identical counts and ordering; tie between bird 3 and cat 3 is alphabetical (bird before cat).
criteria.refutation_attempted: Recomputed word frequencies independently with my own regex+Counter and compared to program output line-by-line; specifically checked the bird/cat count-3 tie is alphabetical (b<c) rather than insertion order. Match exact.

criteria.evidence: `python -m pytest tools/tests/ -q` -> '8 passed in 0.25s', exit=0. `--collect-only` confirms 8 tests collected (>=3).
criteria.refutation_attempted: Ran --collect-only to confirm tests are real and >=3, not a single parametrized stub; 8 distinct test functions collected and all passed.

criteria.evidence: `python tools/wordfreq.py tools/tests/empty.txt 5` -> exit=0, stdout 0 bytes, stderr empty (no Traceback). `wc -c tools/tests/empty.txt` = 0 bytes; file exists.
criteria.refutation_attempted: Verified empty.txt is genuinely zero bytes via wc -c and ls; captured stdout byte count (0) and full stderr separately to catch any traceback text — none present.

criteria.evidence: Provided verify command asserts no duplicate case-variant words; output words=['the','fox','dog','bird','cat','ant','elephant'], exit=0. sample.txt confirmed to contain mixed-case duplicates: the={the,The,THE}, fox={fox,Fox,FOX}, dog, cat, bird.
criteria.refutation_attempted: Went beyond the assertion: enumerated all raw tokens and grouped by lowercased key to prove sample.txt actually contains >1 distinct case form for multiple words (5 words), so the dedup test is non-vacuous. Also confirmed unit test test_count_words_is_case_insensitive collapses The/the/THE/tHe to count 4.

banned_outcomes.evidence: `grep -nE 'socket|urllib|requests|http' tools/wordfreq.py tools/tests/test_wordfreq.py` returned no matches (exit 1). Only imports are re, sys, collections.Counter (wordfreq) and subprocess, sys, pathlib (tests).
`git diff goals/2026-07-07-wordfreq/criteria.json` produced empty output; `git status --porcelain` on the file is empty — unmodified.
